sanskrit:
नान्यं गुणेभ्यः कर्तारं यदा द्रष्टानुपश्यति।
गुणेभ्यश्च परं वेत्ति मद्भावं सोऽधिगच्छति॥
transliteration:
nānyaṃ guṇebhyaḥ kartāraṃ yadā draṣṭānupaśyati |
guṇebhyaś ca paraṃ vetti mad-bhāvaṃ so ’dhigacchati ||
literal_meaning: na—not; anyam—other; guṇebhyaḥ—from the guṇas; kartāram—doer; yadā—when; draṣṭā—the seer; anupaśyati—sees; guṇebhyaḥ—from the guṇas; ca—and; param—beyond; vetti—knows; mat-bhāvam—My being; saḥ—he; adhigacchati—reaches.
contextual_meaning: The moment you realize that every action is merely the play of three mind-moods (guṇas) and that your true Self stands outside them, you stop blaming yourself or others and awaken to a steady, witness-consciousness that is ‘in the world but not of it.’
psychological_state: Cognitive defusion from internal narratives; shift from agentic ego to observing Self (metacognitive awareness).
trigger_scenario: You explode at your child, then catch yourself: 'That wasn’t “me,” that was a surge of tamasik exhaustion and rajasik urgency.' The guilt dissolves, replaced by compassionate regulation.
anchor_text: Watch the three moods move; rest as the untouched witness.
